figures.caption: Figure 1: Multi-Agent Social Systems (MASS) are networked environments where heterogeneous agents exchange information and influence one another over time.
Figure 2: MASS dynamics in MoltBook. Top row: Co-evolving reply-network. Bottom plots: Dynamics of four structural priors.
Table 1: Structural priors of Multi-Agent Social Systems and their connections to social theory. (Expanded table in Appendix A)
Table 4: Kruskal–Wallis (KW) and Mann–Whitney U (MWU) test results across bins. Bonferronicorrected significance indicated. Significance levels: *** p < 0.001.
Figure 3: Statistical summary of MoltBook analysis
Table 2: Expanded of Multi-Agent Social Systems against Single-Task Agentic Systems along four structural properties.
